title: andira racemosa
intro: Andira racemosa, also known as the Brazilian rosewood or andiroba, is a tropical tree native to the Amazon rainforest, characterized by its fragrant, yellow flowers and valuable timber. The medicinal properties of Andira racemosa include its rich oil content, which has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant's oil include pain relief, reduced inflammation, and improved skin health. The herbal preparations of Andira racemosa include cold-pressing the seeds to extract the oil, which can be used topically or added to skincare products, as well as using the wood for its medicinal and timber purposes.
